test_cases.title: Staff Toxicologist (Specialist) - Microplastics Toxicologist
test_cases.company: California public health and environmental agency
test_cases.description: Senior specialist role developing human health risk assessment documents for chemical contaminants in drinking water, with emphasis on microplastics; evaluating toxicology, epidemiology, exposure, and water monitoring data; developing PHGs, notification level recommendations, and health-protective concentrations using dose-response modeling, toxicokinetics, statistical methods, and novel modeling techniques; coordinating with water regulators and responding to peer review and public comments.
test_cases.rationale: Exact target role combining senior regulatory toxicology, microplastics, drinking-water health effects, risk assessment, dose-response modeling, toxicokinetics, and government scientific work products.

test_cases.title: Senior Environmental Health Risk Assessor - Emerging Contaminants
test_cases.description: Develop human health risk assessments for drinking water contaminants including PFAS, microplastics, and other contaminants of emerging concern. Review toxicology and epidemiology studies, perform exposure and dose-response assessments, derive health-protective values, prepare technical reports, and brief regulators and public advisory groups.
test_cases.rationale: Strong adjacent role with the same regulated public-health risk-assessment domain and many target methods, but not the exact gold-standard position.

test_cases.title: Computational Toxicology Scientist - Chemical Risk Assessment
test_cases.description: Apply toxicokinetic models, IVIVE, PBPK, dose-response methods, and computational toxicology tools to evaluate chemical hazard and support regulatory risk assessment for environmental contaminants. Prepare peer-reviewed reports and collaborate with program scientists.
test_cases.rationale: Strong technical overlap in toxicokinetics, dose-response, computational toxicology, and regulatory risk assessment, but lacks drinking-water microplastics focus.

test_cases.title: Environmental Scientist - Contaminants of Emerging Concern
test_cases.description: Support research on contaminants of emerging concern in water systems. Review literature, summarize monitoring data, assist with data visualization, and help prepare technical memos for clients and partner agencies.
test_cases.rationale: Plausible adjacent water and emerging-contaminants role, but generic and missing senior toxicology, health-protective values, dose-response, toxicokinetics, and microplastics-specific regulatory deliverables.

test_cases.title: Senior Stormwater Scientist
test_cases.description: Manage stormwater compliance projects, prepare SWPPPs, conduct NPDES permit support, oversee BMP inspections, coordinate field sampling, and support client reporting for municipal and industrial stormwater programs.
test_cases.rationale: Misleading overlap with water and environment, but wrong job family and task type; compliance, field sampling, and stormwater management are not target risk-assessment toxicology work.

test_cases.title: Air Pollution Monitoring Technical Support Specialist
test_cases.description: Maintain ambient air monitoring equipment, perform data validation, support emissions inventories, conduct field audits, and troubleshoot monitoring stations for regulatory air quality programs.
test_cases.rationale: Government environmental role with broad regulatory and data terms, but wrong medium, wrong task family, and no toxicology risk assessment, drinking-water health effects, or microplastics work.

test_cases.title: Microbiology and Environmental Monitoring Analyst
test_cases.description: Perform microbial environmental monitoring, sample preparation, sterility testing, QA documentation, and routine laboratory analysis under GMP requirements.
test_cases.rationale: Contains environmental monitoring and analyst terms, but is a routine microbiology laboratory quality role rather than senior environmental toxicology or regulatory risk assessment.

test_cases.title: Principal Environmental Risk Assessor
test_cases.description: Senior leadership role in toxicology, ecological risk assessment (ERA), and human health risk assessment (HHRA). Lead complex site investigation, remediation, and redevelopment projects under CERCLA, RCRA, PFAS, sediment, vapor intrusion, and risk-based cleanup projects. Serve as a senior technical resource in toxicology, exposure assessment, fate and transport, conceptual site model development, and risk-based cleanup strategy. Develop ERA and HHRA projects including toxicity criteria, toxicity reference values, exposure pathway analysis, risk characterization, and cleanup levels. Work with USEPA, CalEPA, clients, regulators, business development, proposals, and consulting teams.
test_cases.rationale: Technically credible adjacent role with strong toxicology, HHRA/ERA, exposure, PFAS, CalEPA, and risk-characterization overlap, but it should stay below ideal-fit roles because principal consulting, client management, business development, and cleanup-site practice growth are not the core target profile.

test_cases.title: Environmental Remediation Project Manager
test_cases.description: Lead site remediation projects, Phase I and Phase II environmental site assessments, soil and groundwater investigations, cleanup planning, subcontractor coordination, client management, and regulatory compliance reporting.
test_cases.rationale: Contains environmental, groundwater, and regulatory terms, but the core work is remediation project management rather than toxicology, dose-response, health effects, or microplastics assessment.
